query: We're on .NET 8 with EF Core + Pomelo against OceanBase. Implement a POST /api/orders endpoint that upserts by idempotency key so retries don't double-insert, with the cancellation token flowing through.
Our PaymentService.cs does sync-over-async (.Result) on the Stripe call and deadlocks under load. Rework it to be properly async end to end.
Write a generic repository pattern over EF Core for our .NET 10 service, with FromSqlInterpolated for raw queries and IOptions for connection settings.
Review this C# diff before I merge — correctness, OceanBase optimistic-concurrency handling, and whether error responses leak internal details.
Build a background hosted service in our .NET 8 worker that drains an outbox table every 5s with retry, cancellation on shutdown, and structured logging.
Design the EF Core entity model and Fluent API config for a multi-tenant audit log that partitions by month on OceanBase.
Generate ISTQB-format manual test cases for the password-reset flow: happy path, invalid/expired token, the 8-char boundary, and lockout after 5 attempts.
Here's the PRD for our checkout redesign. Build a risk-based test plan — scope, approach by layer, entry/exit criteria, P0 vs P2.
We use Reqnroll. Turn these acceptance criteria for the loyalty-points story into .feature files plus C# step definition stubs.
Write Playwright E2E tests using the page object model for our .NET storefront — login, add to cart, checkout.
Our /orders API needs p95 < 200ms at 500 RPS. Write the NBomber scenarios — steady-state, spike, and soak.
Scan this .NET solution and tell me which service methods and exception handlers have no test coverage.
We got exec buy-in for a B2B billing revamp. Write a BRD — business problem, measurable goals, stakeholders, in/out of scope, risk register.
Turn this one-pager about a customer loyalty program into a full PRD: personas with JTBD, user journeys, MoSCoW-tagged requirements, NFRs, success metrics.
Decompose this self-service-refunds epic into INVEST user stories using SPIDR, with Gherkin acceptance criteria including negative and authorization cases.
We have 18 feature requests and capacity for 5 this quarter — prioritize with RICE and turn it into a Now/Next/Later roadmap for leadership.
Plan sprint 14: the sprint goal, which stories fit our velocity of 32 points, and the dependencies and risks.
The 'export to CSV' story has no acceptance criteria. Write SMART Given/When/Then ACs covering happy path, empty data, large files, and unauthorized access.

Review this `@Observable` view model — I'm getting actor-isolation warnings and I'm not sure the Sendable conformance on the model I pass into a detached Task is correct under Swift 6 strict concurrency.
Audit this image-cache class for retain cycles — the completion closures capture self strongly and I don't think ARC is ever releasing the cached entries.
Review my SwiftUI settings screen against the iOS 26 HIG — the toolbar treatment, whether the Liquid Glass usage is right, and if the SF Symbols are the correct weights.
Does this custom card layout reflow correctly at the largest Dynamic Type sizes, and is the spacing and control sizing HIG-compliant on macOS Tahoe?
My App Store archive fails with 'no provisioning profile matches' for the distribution build — sort out the signing and the export options so I can ship it.
Swift Package Manager won't resolve after I bumped a dependency — there's a version conflict and the build can't find the module. Fix the package resolution so it builds.
I'm submitting to the App Store next week — audit my PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy and the privacy nutrition labels for completeness against what the app actually collects.
We're adding the Firebase Analytics SDK, which collects device identifiers — what privacy-manifest entries and NSUserTrackingUsageDescription changes do I need before release?
I just built the new onboarding flow — run it in the simulator and confirm each screen renders and the Continue button advances to the next step before I mark this done.
Check how the feed screen looks in the simulator for the empty state and the network-error state — take screenshots and confirm the UI is correct in both.
Run the full unit and integration test suite on the simulator and triage whatever fails so I know what's actually broken.
I refactored the sync layer — run the tests on the simulator to confirm I didn't introduce regressions, and make sure the SwiftData model tests are using XCTestCase.
Our React + TypeScript cart re-renders on every keystroke. Profile it and fix the unnecessary re-renders.
I need a Python script that reads a folder of CSVs and loads them into Postgres with pandas.
Our Swift iOS app crashes on launch after the iOS 18 update — find out why and fix it.
